content: Just as language learners explicitly build reflective vocabulary habits to turn implicit comprehension into explicit, reusable metacognitive knowledge, current LLM self-reflection mechanisms can borrow the high school language teaching framework of structured reflection vocabulary scaffolding to help LLMs turn vague self-critique into standardized, retrievable self-reflection tags stored in dynamic memory. This creates a staged reflection learning pathway that moves from casual self-assessment to structured, reusable reflective practice for autonomous agents.
